title: The discrete mass after a KdV conservative update changes by boundary flux mismatch

source.notes: Domain-facing discrete-mass version of the KdV conservative total-balance theorem.

informal.statement: The discrete mass after a KdV-like conservative update equals the old mass minus scale times the boundary flux mismatch.
assumptions: scale, nonlinear, and dispersion are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state
n is the number of cells in the finite range
discreteMass is the finite sum over the grid range
informal.expected_conclusion: Discrete mass changes only by the KdV boundary flux mismatch.

declarations.name: discreteMass
declarations.statement: def discreteMass (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := totalOn u n

declarations.name: discreteMass_kdvConservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: theorem discreteMass_kdvConservativeUpdate (scale nonlinear dispersion : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : discreteMass (fun i => kdvConservativeUpdate scale nonlinear dispersion u i) n = discreteMass u n - scale * (kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u n - kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u 0)

tasks.input: Translate the discrete-mass balance theorem for the KdV conservative update into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem discreteMass_kdvConservativeUpdate (scale nonlinear dispersion : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : discreteMass (fun i => kdvConservativeUpdate scale nonlinear dispersion u i) n = discreteMass u n - scale * (kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u n - kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion u 0)

tasks.input: Given discreteMass and totalOn_kdvConservativeUpdate, complete the proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding discreteMass and applying totalOn_kdvConservativeUpdate.

tasks.input: Find the KdV theorem that gives total balance before rewriting it as a mass balance.
